---
title: "The Three-Layer Stack: Bundle, App SDK, MAP"
---


The single clearest way to understand these three is that they sit at **different layers of abstraction** and solve **different problems for different people on different days**. Let me walk through what each one is, who owns it, and how they compose.

---

## Conceptual Roles at a Glance

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                                    │
│   MONAI Bundle            →  "The brain"                           │
│   ─────────────────          Pure ML artifact.                     │
│   Who produces it:           Data scientist / ML engineer          │
│   What it contains:          Weights + metadata + declarative      │
│                              preprocessing transforms              │
│   Format:                    A *directory* with JSON configs       │
│   Talks in:                  Tensors, NIfTI, in-memory arrays      │
│   Knows nothing about:       DICOM, PACS, hospital systems         │
│                                                                    │
│   ── wrapped by ──                                                 │
│                                                                    │
│   MONAI Deploy App SDK    →  "The toolkit"                         │
│   ─────────────────────      Python framework (build-time)         │
│   Who uses it:               Deployment engineer                   │
│   What it gives you:         Operator base class, DICOM Operators, │
│                              Application runtime, DAG executor,    │
│                              packaging CLI (`monai-deploy package`)│
│   You write:                 An Application that composes          │
│                              Operators into a pipeline             │
│   Talks in:                  DICOM in, DICOM out                   │
│                                                                    │
│   ── produces ──                                                   │
│                                                                    │
│   MAP                     →  "The shippable product"               │
│   ─────                      OCI container (runtime artifact)      │
│   Who consumes it:           Orchestrator (mercure, Workflow       │
│                              Manager, K8s), SREs, hospital IT      │
│   What it contains:          App SDK runtime + your Application +  │
│                              the Bundle + OS + CUDA + deps         │
│   Format:                    `docker pull ghcr.io/you/app:v1.0`    │
│   Immutable, signable, shippable                                   │
│                                                                    │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Another way to say it: **Bundle is WHAT (the model), App SDK is HOW (the build tool), MAP is WHERE (the deployable unit)**.

---

## The Dependency Direction

The arrows matter — each layer depends on the one above, not the other way around:

```
                 ┌──────────────────┐
                 │   MONAI Bundle   │   ← stands alone, reusable
                 │  (self-contained)│     (you can use it in
                 └────────▲─────────┘      a Jupyter notebook
                          │                or training loop)
                          │ embeds
                          │
                 ┌────────┴─────────┐
                 │ Application      │   ← written using App SDK;
                 │  (Operator DAG)  │     calls the Bundle through
                 │                  │     MonaiBundleInferenceOperator
                 └────────▲─────────┘
                          │
                          │ packaged as
                          │
                 ┌────────┴─────────┐
                 │      MAP         │   ← produced by
                 │ (OCI container)  │     `monai-deploy package …`
                 └──────────────────┘
```

A Bundle is useful on its own — people run them inside Jupyter, inside training pipelines, or inside MLflow. A Bundle *without* a MAP is still a complete ML artifact. But an Application *without* a Bundle is just plumbing, and a MAP *without* an Application is impossible (MAP is literally the packaged form of an Application).

---

## Layer 1 — The MONAI Bundle

### What it is

A **directory on disk** with a fixed layout. That's the whole trick — it's a convention, not a binary format. Any tool that understands the convention can consume it.

```
bundle_lung_seg/
├── configs/
│   ├── metadata.json        ← who, what, version, I/O shapes, license
│   ├── inference.json       ← declarative pre/post/inference pipeline
│   ├── train.json           ← optional — training config
│   └── logging.conf
├── models/
│   ├── model.pt             ← PyTorch weights
│   └── model.onnx           ← optional — portable ONNX export
├── docs/
│   ├── README.md
│   └── license.txt
└── scripts/                 ← optional custom Python code
```

The two files that make this a *Designed Published Language* (in the DDD sense) are `metadata.json` and `inference.json`. They are what make a Bundle **interpretable without reading Python source**.

### `configs/metadata.json` — the model's identity card

```json
{
  "version": "1.0.3",
  "changelog": {
    "1.0.3": "Retrained with 500 additional Rama chest CT cases.",
    "1.0.2": "Fixed RAS orientation bug."
  },
  "monai_version": "1.4.0",
  "pytorch_version": "2.3.1",
  "task": "Lung segmentation from chest CT",
  "description": "3D UNet for left/right lung segmentation on non-contrast chest CT.",
  "authors": "Radiology AI Unit, Ramathibodi Hospital",
  "copyright": "© 2026 Ramathibodi Hospital",
  "data_source": "Internal, IRB-approved (Rama-IRB-2025-XXX)",
  "data_type": "dicom",
  "image_classes": "3D CT, non-contrast",
  "network_data_format": {
    "inputs": {
      "image": {
        "type": "image",
        "format": "hounsfield",
        "modality": "CT",
        "num_channels": 1,
        "spatial_shape": [96, 96, 96],
        "dtype": "float32",
        "value_range": [-1000, 1000],
        "is_patch_data": true
      }
    },
    "outputs": {
      "pred": {
        "type": "image",
        "format": "segmentation",
        "num_channels": 3,
        "spatial_shape": [96, 96, 96],
        "dtype": "float32",
        "channel_def": {"0": "background", "1": "left_lung", "2": "right_lung"}
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Notice `network_data_format` — this is the *machine-readable contract* that separates MONAI Bundle from ONNX. ONNX tells you "input is `float32[1,1,96,96,96]`". Bundle tells you "input is `hounsfield CT` at `[-1000, 1000]` HU, one channel, sliding-window patches of 96³." That is the **preprocessing contract**.

### `configs/inference.json` — the executable preprocessing + inference DAG

This is the file that makes Bundle uniquely valuable for radiology. It declaratively describes the transforms using MONAI's ConfigParser:

```json
{
  "imports": [
    "$import glob",
    "$import os"
  ],
  "bundle_root": ".",
  "ckpt_path": "$@bundle_root + '/models/model.pt'",
  "dataset_dir": "/workspace/input",

  "device": "$torch.device('cuda:0')",
  "network_def": {
    "_target_": "monai.networks.nets.UNet",
    "spatial_dims": 3,
    "in_channels": 1,
    "out_channels": 3,
    "channels": [16, 32, 64, 128, 256],
    "strides": [2, 2, 2, 2]
  },
  "network": "$@network_def.to(@device)",

  "preprocessing": {
    "_target_": "Compose",
    "transforms": [
      {"_target_": "LoadImaged",       "keys": "image"},
      {"_target_": "EnsureChannelFirstd","keys": "image"},
      {"_target_": "Orientationd",     "keys": "image", "axcodes": "RAS"},
      {"_target_": "Spacingd",         "keys": "image", "pixdim": [1.5, 1.5, 2.0]},
      {"_target_": "ScaleIntensityRanged",
         "keys": "image", "a_min": -1000, "a_max": 400,
         "b_min": 0.0, "b_max": 1.0, "clip": true},
      {"_target_": "CropForegroundd",  "keys": "image", "source_key": "image"},
      {"_target_": "EnsureTyped",      "keys": "image"}
    ]
  },

  "inferer": {
    "_target_": "SlidingWindowInferer",
    "roi_size": [96, 96, 96],
    "sw_batch_size": 4,
    "overlap": 0.5
  },

  "postprocessing": {
    "_target_": "Compose",
    "transforms": [
      {"_target_": "Activationsd", "keys": "pred", "softmax": true},
      {"_target_": "AsDiscreted",  "keys": "pred", "argmax": true},
      {"_target_": "Invertd",      "keys": "pred",
         "transform": "@preprocessing", "orig_keys": "image"},
      {"_target_": "SaveImaged",   "keys": "pred",
         "output_dir": "@output_dir", "output_postfix": "seg"}
    ]
  }
}
```

Read that carefully — every field labelled `"_target_"` is a Python class name. The ConfigParser instantiates them at runtime. Result: **the preprocessing pipeline is data, not code**. You could swap `pixdim` to `[1.0, 1.0, 1.0]` by editing JSON, with no recompile. You could lint it, diff it in Git, attach it to an FDA submission as the "description of preprocessing."

### Using a Bundle alone (no MAP, no App SDK)

```python
from monai.bundle import ConfigParser

parser = ConfigParser()
parser.read_config("bundle_lung_seg/configs/inference.json")
parser.read_meta("bundle_lung_seg/configs/metadata.json")

# Instantiate everything declared in JSON
preprocessing  = parser.get_parsed_content("preprocessing")
network        = parser.get_parsed_content("network")
inferer        = parser.get_parsed_content("inferer")
postprocessing = parser.get_parsed_content("postprocessing")

# Load checkpoint
network.load_state_dict(torch.load(parser.get_parsed_content("ckpt_path")))
network.eval()

# Run on a NIfTI file
data = preprocessing({"image": "/path/to/ct.nii.gz"})
with torch.no_grad():
    data["pred"] = inferer(data["image"].unsqueeze(0).to("cuda"), network)
postprocessing(data)  # writes output NIfTI
```

At this level you are **outside the hospital context**. No DICOM, no PACS. This is what a data scientist works with at a desk.

---

## Layer 2 — The MONAI Deploy App SDK

### What it is

A **Python framework** (`pip install monai-deploy-app-sdk`) that gives you three things:

1. A **runtime** — the class `Application` with a `compose()` method and an execution engine that walks a DAG.
2. A library of **pre-built Operators** — especially DICOM-aware ones that a plain Bundle cannot provide.
3. A **packaging CLI** — `monai-deploy package` that turns your Application into a MAP.

The Operator is the core abstraction. Each Operator is a Python class with typed inputs and outputs:

```
          ┌─────────────────────────┐
          │       Operator          │
          │  (user-defined or       │
          │   built-in)             │
          │                         │
   in ───▶│  compute() method       │────▶ out
          │                         │
          │  • declares in/out types│
          │  • runs as one DAG node │
          └─────────────────────────┘
```

### The built-in Operator catalogue (the valuable part)

These are what a Bundle alone cannot give you:

```
Category          Built-in Operator
────────────────  ─────────────────────────────────────────────
DICOM ingress     DICOMDataLoaderOperator
                  DICOMSeriesSelectorOperator
                  DICOMSeriesToVolumeOperator

Inference core    MonaiBundleInferenceOperator   ← glue layer!
                  InferenceOperator               (low-level)

DICOM egress      DICOMSegmentationWriterOperator (SEG / Sup 111)
                  DICOMTextSRWriterOperator       (SR TID 1500)
                  PublisherOperator               (push to PACS)

Utilities         PublisherOperator, ClaraVizOperator
```

The `MonaiBundleInferenceOperator` is the **bridge** between Layer 1 and Layer 2. It takes a Bundle path and exposes it as an Operator:

```python
# Inside the App SDK, conceptually:
class MonaiBundleInferenceOperator(Operator):
    def __init__(self, bundle_path):
        self.parser = ConfigParser()
        self.parser.read_config(f"{bundle_path}/configs/inference.json")
        self.preprocessing  = self.parser.get_parsed_content("preprocessing")
        self.network        = self.parser.get_parsed_content("network")
        self.inferer        = self.parser.get_parsed_content("inferer")
        self.postprocessing = self.parser.get_parsed_content("postprocessing")

    def compute(self, op_input, op_output, context):
        volume = op_input.get("image")       # from DICOMSeriesToVolume
        data   = self.preprocessing({"image": volume})
        data["pred"] = self.inferer(data["image"], self.network)
        data   = self.postprocessing(data)
        op_output.set(data["pred"], label="seg")
```

### Writing an Application — the full example

Here's a complete Rama lung-segmentation app. This file is what you commit to the `ramaai-dev/rama-lung-seg-map` repo:

```python
# app.py
import logging
from pathlib import Path

from monai.deploy.core import Application, env, resource
from monai.deploy.operators.dicom_data_loader_operator \
    import DICOMDataLoaderOperator
from monai.deploy.operators.dicom_series_selector_operator \
    import DICOMSeriesSelectorOperator
from monai.deploy.operators.dicom_series_to_volume_operator \
    import DICOMSeriesToVolumeOperator
from monai.deploy.operators.monai_bundle_inference_operator \
    import MonaiBundleInferenceOperator, IOMapping
from monai.deploy.operators.dicom_seg_writer_operator \
    import DICOMSegmentationWriterOperator, SegmentDescription


@resource(cpu=2, gpu=1, memory="8Gi")
@env(pip_packages=["highdicom>=0.22", "pydicom>=2.4"])
class RamaLungSegApp(Application):
    """Rama Lung Segmentation — reads CT series, emits DICOM SEG to PACS."""

    name        = "rama-lung-seg"
    description = "Left/right lung segmentation on non-contrast chest CT"
    version     = "1.0.3"

    def compose(self):
        logging.info("Composing Rama Lung Seg Application")

        # ── DICOM selection rule: pick axial non-contrast chest CT ──────
        selection_rules = """{
          "selections": [{
            "name": "CT Chest Non-contrast",
            "conditions": {
              "Modality":          "(?i)CT",
              "BodyPartExamined":  "(?i)chest|thorax",
              "ContrastBolusAgent":"^$"
            }
          }]
        }"""

        loader   = DICOMDataLoaderOperator(self, name="loader")
        selector = DICOMSeriesSelectorOperator(
            self, rules=selection_rules, name="selector"
        )
        to_vol   = DICOMSeriesToVolumeOperator(self, name="to_vol")

        # ── The Bundle becomes an Operator here ─────────────────────────
        bundle_op = MonaiBundleInferenceOperator(
            self,
            app_context=self.context,
            bundle_path=Path(__file__).parent / "bundle_lung_seg",
            input_mapping=[IOMapping("image", "nifti", ("numpy.ndarray",))],
            output_mapping=[IOMapping("pred",  "image", ("numpy.ndarray",))],
            name="bundle_inference",
        )

        # ── DICOM SEG egress (Supplement 111 via highdicom) ─────────────
        seg_writer = DICOMSegmentationWriterOperator(
            self,
            segment_descriptions=[
                SegmentDescription(
                    segment_label="Left Lung",
                    segmented_property_category=("T-D0050", "SRT", "Anatomical Structure"),
                    segmented_property_type=("T-28000", "SRT", "Left Lung"),
                ),
                SegmentDescription(
                    segment_label="Right Lung",
                    segmented_property_category=("T-D0050", "SRT", "Anatomical Structure"),
                    segmented_property_type=("T-29000", "SRT", "Right Lung"),
                ),
            ],
            name="seg_writer",
        )

        # ── Wire the DAG ────────────────────────────────────────────────
        self.add_flow(loader,    selector,    {"dicom_study_list": "dicom_study_list"})
        self.add_flow(selector,  to_vol,      {"study_selected_series_list": "study_selected_series_list"})
        self.add_flow(to_vol,    bundle_op,   {"image": "image"})
        self.add_flow(bundle_op, seg_writer,  {"pred": "seg_image"})
        self.add_flow(selector,  seg_writer,  {"study_selected_series_list": "study_selected_series_list"})


if __name__ == "__main__":
    RamaLungSegApp(do_run=True)
```

The DAG you built:

```
  loader ──▶ selector ──▶ to_vol ──▶ bundle_op ──▶ seg_writer
                 │                                     ▲
                 └─────────────────────────────────────┘
                   (series metadata threads through
                    so SEG inherits Study/Frame-of-Reference)
```

Two things worth noticing. First, the `MonaiBundleInferenceOperator(..., bundle_path=...)` is the *exact seam* between Layer 1 and Layer 2 — everything to the left of it is DICOM-land (App SDK's job), everything inside it is tensor-land (Bundle's job). Second, the two decorators `@resource` and `@env` are hints consumed by the packaging CLI when building the OCI image.

---

## Layer 3 — The MAP (the packaged output)

### What the build command does

```bash
monai-deploy package app.py \
    --tag ghcr.io/ramaai-dev/rama-lung-seg:1.0.3 \
    --platform x86_64-cuda \
    --log-level INFO \
    -c app.yaml
```

This single command produces an OCI image. Under the hood it:

1. Reads `@resource` and `@env` decorators to size the container.
2. Copies `app.py` + `bundle_lung_seg/` into `/opt/holoscan/app/`.
3. Bakes a `/etc/monai/app.json` manifest describing name, version, inputs (DICOM series), outputs (DICOM SEG), resources, timeout.
4. Writes an `ENTRYPOINT` that invokes the App SDK runtime with `app.py`.
5. Wraps it all in a CUDA-enabled base image.

The resulting image structure (simplified):

```
ghcr.io/ramaai-dev/rama-lung-seg:1.0.3
│
├── /opt/nvidia/monai/                  ← App SDK runtime
├── /opt/holoscan/app/
│     ├── app.py                        ← your Application
│     ├── bundle_lung_seg/              ← your Bundle (complete)
│     │     ├── configs/…
│     │     └── models/…
│     └── requirements.txt
├── /etc/monai/
│     └── app.json                      ← MAP manifest (contract)
├── /var/monai/input/                   ← mount point for DICOM in
├── /var/monai/output/                  ← mount point for DICOM out
└── ENTRYPOINT ["python", "-m", "monai.deploy.runner", "/opt/holoscan/app/app.py"]
```

### Running the MAP

Because it's OCI, any container runtime can execute it. The contract is **DICOM in a directory → DICOM out a directory**:

```bash
docker run --gpus all --rm \
  -v /tmp/input:/var/monai/input:ro \
  -v /tmp/output:/var/monai/output \
  ghcr.io/ramaai-dev/rama-lung-seg:1.0.3
```

mercure dispatches to this identical command when a rule matches. The Workflow Manager dispatches to it. A Kubernetes Job dispatches to it. Same interface, many callers — this is why MAP works as a Published Language for the deployment context.

### The `app.json` manifest

```json
{
  "apiVersion": "1.0",
  "command": ["python", "-m", "monai.deploy.runner", "/opt/holoscan/app/app.py"],
  "environment": {
    "HOLOSCAN_INPUT_PATH":  "/var/monai/input",
    "HOLOSCAN_OUTPUT_PATH": "/var/monai/output",
    "HOLOSCAN_MODEL_PATH":  "/opt/holoscan/models"
  },
  "input": {
    "path":    "input/",
    "formats": ["dicom"]
  },
  "output": {
    "path":    "output/",
    "formats": ["dicom-seg"]
  },
  "resources": {
    "cpu": 2,
    "gpu": 1,
    "memory": "8Gi",
    "gpu_memory": "8Gi"
  },
  "timeout": 300,
  "version": "1.0.3",
  "working_directory": "/var/monai/working"
}
```

This manifest is what mercure and the Workflow Manager read to decide *how* to schedule the MAP — how much GPU memory to reserve, how long before timing out, where to mount inputs.

---

## The Full Lifecycle — Build Time vs Run Time

Here's the picture that ties it all together, with the two distinct phases clearly separated:

```
  ═══════════════════ BUILD TIME (data scientist + deployment engineer) ═══════════════════

  ┌──────────────────┐       ┌──────────────────┐       ┌──────────────────┐
  │ 1. Train model   │       │ 2. Export as     │       │ 3. Write App     │
  │ PyTorch training │──────▶│    Bundle        │──────▶│    using SDK     │
  │ on Rama CT data  │       │ (JSON configs +  │       │ (DICOM Operators │
  │                  │       │  weights)        │       │  + Bundle Op)    │
  └──────────────────┘       └──────────────────┘       └────────┬─────────┘
                                      │                           │
                                      │                           ▼
                                      │             ┌──────────────────────┐
                                      │             │ 4. monai-deploy      │
                                      └────────────▶│    package app.py    │
                                                    │    → OCI image       │
                                                    └────────┬─────────────┘
                                                             │
                                                             ▼
                                                    ┌──────────────────────┐
                                                    │ 5. cosign sign +     │
                                                    │    push to registry  │
                                                    │    (ghcr.io/...)     │
                                                    └──────────────────────┘

  ═══════════════════ RUN TIME (orchestrator + hospital) ═══════════════════════════════════

   PACS                     Orchestrator              MAP Container                  PACS
   ────                     ────────────              ─────────────                  ────
     │                           │                          │                          │
     │  New CT study             │                          │                          │
     ├──────────────────────────▶│                          │                          │
     │  (DIMSE C-STORE)          │                          │                          │
     │                           │ rule: Modality=CT,       │                          │
     │                           │       BodyPart=CHEST     │                          │
     │                           │   → route to rama-lung-seg                          │
     │                           │                          │                          │
     │                           │ docker run               │                          │
     │                           ├─────────────────────────▶│                          │
     │                           │ (mount input DICOM)      │                          │
     │                           │                          │                          │
     │                           │              ┌───────────▼──────────┐               │
     │                           │              │ App SDK runtime      │               │
     │                           │              │ executes Operator DAG│               │
     │                           │              │   loader → selector  │               │
     │                           │              │   → to_vol →         │               │
     │                           │              │   [Bundle: transforms│               │
     │                           │              │    → UNet → post]    │               │
     │                           │              │   → seg_writer       │               │
     │                           │              └───────────┬──────────┘               │
     │                           │                          │                          │
     │                           │  DICOM SEG in /output/   │                          │
     │                           │◀─────────────────────────┤                          │
     │                           │                          │                          │
     │                           │   STOW-RS                │                          │
     │  DICOM SEG back to PACS   │                          │                          │
     │◀──────────────────────────┤                          │                          │
     │                           │                          │                          │
```

---

## Why the Separation Matters (DDD framing)

Because you care about software architecture philosophy, here's the framing that justifies the three layers:

**Bundle = Domain Model.** Pure, framework-agnostic-ish, talks in the language of medical imaging (HU, spacing, modality, segmentation classes). It has no infrastructure dependencies — no DICOM, no filesystem layouts, no container runtime. A radiologist-scientist can reason about it without caring how it gets deployed.

**App SDK = Application Layer.** It is the orchestration of use cases, in Evans's sense. It composes domain objects (Bundles) with infrastructure adapters (DICOM operators) into a coherent workflow. It does *not* contain domain logic — `MonaiBundleInferenceOperator` is a thin wrapper, not a reimplementation. The DICOM Operators are *adapters* at the bounded-context boundary.

**MAP = Published Language / Infrastructure Artifact.** It is the contract the deployment context publishes to the orchestration context. Orchestrators do not read Python source — they read the `app.json` manifest and invoke `docker run`. The OCI digest is the configuration item that IEC 62304 and FDA PCCP want to pin.

The payoff: you can **swap any one layer without touching the others**.

```
  Keep Bundle, change App          Keep App, swap Bundle          Keep MAP, replace runtime
  ───────────────────────          ─────────────────────          ─────────────────────────
  Same model, but new              Same DICOM pipeline,           MAP is OCI — runs on Docker,
  input: now served over           but model upgraded from        Podman, containerd, K8s,
  a REST API instead of            v1.0 → v1.1 (retrained)        OpenShift. Zero code change
  DICOM ingress.                                                  in the Application.
```

---

## Practical Use Cases for Your Platform

**Case A — Rama-developed model, in-house deployment.** Write Bundle → write Application → `monai-deploy package` → push to `ghcr.io/ramaai-dev/` → mercure routes studies to it. You own all three layers. This is your primary workflow.

**Case B — Reuse a MONAI Model Zoo Bundle.** Pull `monai/spleen_ct_segmentation` Bundle from the Zoo. Write a thin Application that wraps it with your Rama-specific DICOM selection rules. Package as your own MAP. You own layer 2 & 3; you borrow layer 1.

**Case C — Vendor ships a MAP.** Vendor hands you `vendor/stroke-ct:2.1`. You don't care what's inside. mercure or the Workflow Manager runs it. You own layer 3 execution only.

**Case D — Research without deployment.** Data scientist writes a Bundle, runs it from a Jupyter notebook via `ConfigParser`, iterates on preprocessing. No MAP, no App SDK needed. Once ready, promote to Case A. This is the "Bundle alone is useful" path.
